author: @marcomikkers7310
text: Basically standing EM waves :) EM radiation is nothing more than oscillating electric and magnetic fields. Very cool video! How about a superconducting LC circuit could this oscillate indefinitely?

author: @lubbock2704
text: It's also possible to create a binary digital computer from only tank circuits, known as a Parametron. My current project is to build one such computer.

author: @kevinleebailey
text: An AM radio loop antenna is a giant tank circuit.

author: @allenhargis4317
text: great explanation.  Capacitors can be used in so many more ways.  Tesla created the 3 phase motor for a paycheck but his best work was with high frequency unidirectional impulses.  You can get more interesting effects with impulses than with alternating current.  also changing the geometry of capacitors can change their uses.  plate capacitors in a moving magnetic field can capture quite a bit of power too.

author: @gerryjamesedwards1227
text: Making your own with One unknown and one known component can help you find the value of the unknown component. You can give the parallel combination a burst of high frequency, say from the rising edge of a square wave, and then measure the period of the ringing of the circuit on a 'scope and use that to calculate the values of the unknown element.

author: @Ken00001010
text: Very nice explanation. It is like hitting a bell or tuning fork. It does not matter how hard you hit it, it rings the same tone.

author: @Kangsteri
text: I love this topic <3 There should be yearly international circuit tuning competition on who can make the Super joule ringer looper style system run for longest time, by any means necessary.
